From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mountain in New South Wales, Australia

| Bushy Mountain | |
| --- | --- |
| [Bushy Mountain is located in New South Wales](/wiki/File:Australia_New_South_Wales_relief_location_map.png "Bushy Mountain is located in New South Wales") Bushy Mountain  Bushy Mountain  Location in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") | |
| Highest point | |
| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [30°18′24″S 150°12′26″E﻿ / ﻿30.30667°S 150.20722°E﻿ / -30.30667; 150.20722](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Bushy_Mountain&params=30_18_24_S_150_12_26_E_type:mountain_region:AU_scale:100000) |
| Geography | |
| Location | [North West Slopes](/wiki/North_West_Slopes "North West Slopes"), [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") |
| [Parent range](/wiki/Mountain_range "Mountain range") | [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range"), [Great Dividing Range](/wiki/Great_Dividing_Range "Great Dividing Range") |

**Bushy Mountain**, a [mountain](/wiki/Mountain "Mountain") of the [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range"), a spur of the [Great Dividing Range](/wiki/Great_Dividing_Range "Great Dividing Range"), is located in the [North West Slopes](/wiki/North_West_Slopes "North West Slopes") region of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). Busy Mountain is situated east of [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri "Narrabri") within the [Mount Kaputar National Park](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar_National_Park "Mount Kaputar National Park").

With an elevation of 1,260 metres (4,130 ft) [above sea level](/wiki/Australian_Height_Datum "Australian Height Datum"), Bushy Mount is part of the remnants of the Nandewar [extinct volcano](/wiki/Extinct_volcano "Extinct volcano") that ceased activity about 17 million years ago after 4 million years of activity.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
